Alexandra Byrum joined Bonner Center Civic Engagement (CCE) April 2011.
Alexandra leads communications efforts manages community relationships, educational programs, Wilton Companies Gallery exhibitions.
Before joining CCE, museum educator Chrysler Museum taught history photography Dominion University Tidewater Community College's Visual Arts Center.
Previously, served associate marketing manager Smithsonian Institution during opening National Museum American Indian reopening Smithsonian American Museum National Portrait Gallery.

France ordered police patrols stepped Tuesday after least three people were killed wounded explosions near finish line Boston Marathon.
France's president expressed solidarity with
Interior Minister Manuel Valls asked local officials security forces reinforce without delay presence patrols" across country, statement from ministry said.
People "asked vigilant suspicious parcels abandoned luggage concerned without giving panic".
Boston police immediately whether explosions were part terrorist attack, marathon organizers said twin bombing.
France been under heightened security measures following military intervention Mali earlier this year.
government raised country's national alert system, Plan Vigipirate, "reinforced red", second highest warning level after receiving non-specific threats from various Islamist militant groups.
Since alert system raised "reinforced red", measures have been taken protect public institutions increase security around major transport hubs such capital's train stations airports.
France only ever once raised security level highest level "crimson", which during Islamist gunman Mohamed Merah's shooting spree March last year.
Early Tuesday, French President François Hollande voiced "France's complete solidarity with American authorities people" following deadly blasts Boston Marathon.
statement, head state expressed "his very strong feelings following explosions that city Boston" that left three dead more than injured.
tragic circumstances, head state offers condolences families victims expresses France's complete solidarity with American authorities people," statement from Elysée said.
